AI-Powered Troll Farms and the Battle for Truth in the Philippines

AI is changing the game for troll farms in the Philippines, making disinformation faster, cheaper, and harder to detect. As the 2025 elections approach, the lines between truth and deception blur. How do we fight back?

Scroll long enough on social media, and something feels off. A flood of comments, eerily similar, flooding every post about politics. New accounts pushing the same narrative. It’s not an accident. It’s not organic. It’s engineered. The Philippines, with its 92.5 million social media users, has become fertile ground for disinformation. Troll farms now have AI on their side. AI has made disinformation faster, cheaper, and harder to detect. Bots pose as real users, AI-generated articles spread lies, and deepfakes make the impossible look real. This isn’t just about elections. It’s about control—over public opinion, over history, over what people believe to be true. And because we’re not paying attention, it’s happening again.

The Rise of Troll Farms in the Philippines
Troll farms don’t operate for free. They exist because someone funds them. Politicians, businesses, and powerful figures pay for influence. They need an army—one that can flood social media with support, silence critics, and manipulate public perception. And in the Philippines, that army is cheap. The country’s low labor costs make it easy to hire operators who manage fake accounts, infiltrate discussions, and push pre-written disinformation. A single troll can run dozens of accounts, pretending to be different people, each one programmed to spread a specific message.

But trolls alone can only do so much. When Rodrigo Duterte’s administration used troll farms to drown out critics and manufacture public approval, the strategy was simple: Make online dissent invisible and amplify voices that supported them. That model has since been adopted by other political groups, evolving from paid commenters to automated disinformation networks. And now, AI has taken it to the next level. What once required thousands of real people can now be done with bots—cheaper, faster, and on a much larger scale. Fake engagement. Fake support. Fake outrage. And with the 2025 elections approaching, the people funding these troll farms are already preparing for war.

How AI is Supercharging Troll Farms
Troll farms used to rely on human operators, each controlling multiple fake accounts. It was messy, time-consuming, and limited by manpower. Now, AI does the heavy lifting. Disinformation has become automated, scalable, and dangerously convincing. With AI, a single operator can flood social media with thousands of posts that look like they came from real people. Comments feel authentic. Fake news articles read like professional journalism. And most alarmingly, videos and voices can be faked with stunning accuracy.

Here’s how AI is reshaping disinformation in the Philippines:

AI-Powered Bots That Mimic Real Users
Bots no longer sound robotic. They engage in discussions, reply to comments, and even create posts that sound human (RAND). Some are designed to argue, others to agree, but their goal is the same—control the conversation (Heinz College - Carnegie Mellon University). Once enough bots interact, they create the illusion of majority opinion—making lies look like popular beliefs (CISA).

Generative AI Writing Fake News at Scale
AI tools like ChatGPT can mass-produce disinformation with human-like fluency (NATO). Fake accounts use AI-generated posts to flood social media, making real information harder to find (International Journal of Social Sciences). These AI-generated posts target specific groups, creating content that manipulates their beliefs and emotions (Fraud Blocker).

Deepfakes and Synthetic Media
AI can generate fake videos and audio recordings, making people say things they never did (East Asia Forum). In politics, deepfakes can be used to spread scandals, fake endorsements, or discredit critics (Modern War Institute). One recent example: a deepfake audio of President Marcos Jr. ordering a military attack—a fabricated event that fooled many (ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute).

Social Media Manipulation Strategies
AI doesn’t just create content—it ensures it spreads. Here’s how troll farms use AI to bury dissenting voices and dominate online spaces:
✔ Click/Like Farming – AI-powered bots flood posts with likes and shares to artificially inflate engagement. A paid influencer’s post or a fake news story can suddenly look widely accepted when in reality, it’s a staged operation (Turing Institute).
✔ Hashtag Hijacking – Trending topics get hijacked as bots insert disinformation into high-traffic conversations. A political discussion about government policies can suddenly be filled with off-topic but highly engaging propaganda, diverting attention away from real issues (Channel News Asia).
✔ Repost Networks – Coordinated accounts instantly repost from a central source, flooding platforms with identical content. This tricks algorithms into promoting the content to even more users, burying organic discussions (The Diplomat).
✔ Mass Reporting to Silence Critics – AI-powered bots mass-report posts from journalists, activists, or dissenting voices. Social media platforms, relying on automated moderation, often suspend these accounts before investigating, effectively silencing opposition (Reuters Institute).
✔ Manipulating Comments to Control Perception – AI can flood the comments section of viral posts with manufactured public opinion. If a government official gets criticized online, an army of bots will flood the comments with praise and counterarguments, shifting perception in their favor (Human Rights Pulse).

Troll farms don’t just spread lies. They erase the truth by flooding the internet with noise. And with AI on their side, it’s happening faster than ever.

Who Troll Farms Target and What They Push
AI-powered troll farms don’t just spread lies randomly. Every post, comment, and manipulated trend has a target and a purpose. They aim for people who pose a threat. They amplify issues that divide society. And they rewrite history and current events to shape public perception.

Who’s in the Crosshairs?
Political Opponents: Discredit them, fabricate scandals, and suppress their reach (ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute).
Journalists & Activists: Silence investigations and smear reputations (Reuters Institute).
Religious & Ethnic Groups: Exploit divisions and stir conflict (RAND).
Academics & Historians: Bury facts that challenge the official version of events (The Diplomat).

AI-powered troll farms excel at targeting individuals, whether it’s a politician on the rise or a journalist uncovering corruption. They don’t just spread negative posts—they weaponize deepfakes, AI-generated articles, and manipulated statistics to make attacks seem more credible. For example, after investigative reports exposed government-linked corruption, journalists in the Philippines faced massive waves of AI-driven harassment—fake posts, doctored screenshots, and coordinated smear campaigns (Human Rights Pulse). Once the target is discredited, the next step is to control the discussion.

What Disinformation Is Being Pushed?
Historical Revisionism: Whitewash past abuses and make controversial figures seem heroic (The Diplomat).
Political Propaganda: Boost favored candidates and manipulate public perception (Fraud Blocker).
Exploiting Social Issues (Crime, Poverty, etc.): Create fear, push authoritarian solutions, or divide communities (RAND).

✔ Historical Revisionism: Troll farms rewrite history by erasing uncomfortable truths or over-glorifying past administrations. AI-generated content is used to downplay human rights abuses or inflate economic successes, particularly with the Marcos era (ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute).
✔ Political Propaganda: Whether it’s amplifying a leader’s “achievements” or spreading baseless corruption claims about an opponent, AI-driven disinformation floods social media in favor of those funding the troll farms (Fraud Blocker).
✔ Exploiting Social Issues: Fear is a powerful tool. AI-generated posts inflate crime statistics, fabricate violent incidents, or push divisive rhetoric about social problems to justify crackdowns or authoritarian policies (The Diplomat).

AI-Powered Lies Shape Public Opinion
AI makes disinformation look real. And when enough people believe it, it becomes reality. This is why troll farms focus on controlling the conversation—not just by spreading false information but by burying the truth under AI-generated noise. With the 2025 elections ahead, these tactics aren’t slowing down. They’re getting more sophisticated. And without awareness, millions of Filipinos won’t even realize they’re being manipulated.

How to Detect AI-Powered Troll Farms
AI-powered troll farms are designed to blend in. They use fake profiles, bot networks, and mass-produced content to manipulate online spaces without looking obvious. But they leave clues.

Suspicious Account Characteristics:
Recently created accounts: Many troll accounts appear just before elections or major political events.
AI-generated or stolen profile pictures: Many use AI-created faces or stolen photos to seem real.
Suspicious following patterns: They follow and are followed by accounts that interact only with each other, forming coordinated networks.
Impersonation of real people: Some fake accounts pose as journalists, activists, or regular users to infiltrate discussions.
Paid verification marks: Some accounts purchase blue checkmarks to gain credibility and avoid content moderation filters.

Content Red Flags:
Repetitive messaging: Troll accounts post the same talking points across multiple platforms.
Emotionally charged language: The goal is to trigger anger, fear, or outrage, not inform.
Amplification of known propagandists: They frequently share and retweet specific disinformation sources.
Unusual posting frequency: Bots post at unnatural speeds, sometimes hundreds of times per day.
Identical or generic content: Some AI-generated posts feel unnatural, overly polished, or repetitive.
Inconsistent language patterns: AI is improving, but some posts still have awkward phrasing or unnatural grammar.

Behavioral Cues of Troll Activity:
Coordinated posting: Multiple accounts post the same message within minutes of each other.
Sudden shifts in discussions: A topic starts trending, and trolls flood the comments with distractions or counter-narratives.
Targeting influential individuals: Journalists, academics, and politicians face sudden waves of harassment from suspicious accounts.
Hashtag hijacking: Trolls flood trending hashtags with off-topic disinformation to drown out organic discussions.
Mass reporting campaigns: Trolls coordinate to report specific posts, leading to temporary or permanent bans for real users.

How to Protect Yourself From AI-Powered Disinformation:
Question the source: Investigate where the information is coming from. Check if it’s being repeated by credible news outlets.
Seek multiple perspectives: Don’t rely on a single post or comment thread—compare different sources before forming an opinion.
Use fact-checking resources: Platforms like Tsek.ph and Rappler’s fact-checking service help verify information in real time.
Watch for AI-generated patterns: If a post feels too polished, check if other accounts are posting the exact same thing. AI-generated disinformation often lacks personal details or originality.
Don’t engage with obvious trolls: Many troll accounts thrive on outrage and engagement—blocking or reporting them denies them visibility.

Troll Farms Count on You Not Noticing
AI-powered disinformation works because it looks real. Troll farms flood social media with content that feels organic, sounds familiar, and blends in with real discussions. But the more people recognize their tactics, the less effective they become. Filipinos aren’t powerless against AI-powered lies—but awareness is the first step.

How to Counter AI-Powered Troll Farms
AI-powered troll farms are escalating faster than efforts to stop them. Fake accounts are created faster than they’re banned. Deepfakes spread before fact-checkers can debunk them. And as AI continues to improve, detecting fabricated content is becoming harder by the day. But while disinformation is evolving, so are the efforts to fight back. Governments, journalists, and fact-checking organizations are working to expose and limit the damage, but progress remains slow. At the same time, ordinary Filipinos must take responsibility—learning how to identify and resist AI-powered manipulation.

Detection and Monitoring Efforts:
Disinformation thrives because it hides in plain sight. Detecting AI-generated propaganda requires tools that can analyze patterns, identify bot behavior, and trace the origins of false narratives. Some of the most active efforts in the Philippines include:
Rappler’s AI-Powered Disinformation Tracking: Since the Duterte administration, Rappler has used AI tools to map disinformation networks, uncovering how troll farms manipulate online discussions. Their research has shown that troll accounts coordinate their attacks to make dissenting voices seem unpopular or “wrong” (Rappler).
Tsek.ph’s Fact-Checking Coalition: A collaboration of Philippine fact-checkers, Tsek.ph provides real-time verification of viral claims. Its researchers work to debunk deepfakes, manipulated videos, and AI-generated misinformation, but they face a critical challenge: by the time a false claim is corrected, millions have already seen and believed it (Tsek.ph).
Vera Files – A Philippine fact-checking organization that actively debunks disinformation, tracks false narratives, and provides guides on how to spot fake news (Vera Files).
Philippine Center for Investigative Journalism (PCIJ) – An independent media organization known for exposing corruption and digital propaganda. It conducts deep investigations into how troll farms are funded (PCIJ).
Facebook’s Takedown of Troll Networks: Meta has removed hundreds of troll farm accounts linked to Philippine disinformation campaigns. However, these takedowns are often reactive—by the time Facebook acts, new accounts have already replaced the old ones (GMA Network).
AI-Powered Detection Systems: Researchers worldwide are developing AI that can spot deepfakes, repetitive AI-generated content, and bot-driven manipulation. But AI-generated disinformation is always evolving, and detection tools are struggling to keep up (RAND).

Legislative and Platform Interventions:
Social Media Crackdowns That Fall Short: Facebook and X (formerly Twitter) have removed thousands of fake accounts, yet disinformation continues to spread unchecked (Channel News Asia).
Proposed Social Media Regulations: Some lawmakers in the Philippines have pushed for mandatory ID verification for social media users. However, critics warn this could endanger activists and whistleblowers (The Diplomat).
Governments Pressuring Big Tech: The Philippine government has joined other nations in calling on Meta, TikTok, and YouTube to strengthen content moderation (Reuters Institute).
Meta’s Abandonment of Fact-Checking: In 2024, Meta scaled back its fact-checking operations. The result? AI-generated propaganda now faces even less resistance on Facebook (GMA Network).

Digital Literacy and Public Awareness:
Filipinos are on their own when it comes to protecting themselves from AI-driven disinformation. Some steps include:
Media Literacy in Schools: Universities and NGOs are working to train young Filipinos to recognize disinformation tactics (ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute).
Fact-Checking Initiatives: Independent organizations like Tsek.ph and Rappler provide free resources to verify information (Rappler).
Community-Based Awareness Campaigns: Organizations are working at the grassroots level to teach people about AI-generated fake news (Human Rights Pulse).

Challenges in Fighting AI-Powered Disinformation:
✔ AI Evolves Too Fast
✔ Platforms Profit from Engagement
✔ Legal Loopholes Exist
✔ Distrust in Fact-Checking

Fighting Back Starts with Awareness
The best defense against AI-powered propaganda isn’t stricter policies or improved moderation—it’s critical thinking. Filipinos need to question what they see online, especially when a post triggers outrage or presents an argument that feels too perfectly written. Troll farms don’t just manufacture lies; they shape conversations, drown out dissenting voices, and manipulate emotions to steer public perception.

Unmasking the AI Arsenal of Troll Farms: A Deep Dive into Disinformation Technology
AI-powered disinformation isn’t just spreading faster—it’s becoming harder to detect. Troll farms aren’t just relying on human labor anymore. They’ve automated their operations with AI-powered tools that generate fake content, manipulate engagement, and create deepfakes that distort reality.

The AI Tools Behind Troll Operations:
1. AI-Powered Content Generation: Fake News & AI-Written Propaganda, Synthetic Profile Creation, Deepfake Technology.
2. Automated Engagement & Algorithm Manipulation: AI-Controlled Bots, Mass Reporting & Hashtag Hijacking.
3. AI-Generated Deepfakes & Video Manipulation.

Software That Powers Disinformation:
Content Generation:
Faker.js – Generates realistic fake names and data (Faker.js).
Mockaroo – Produces large volumes of fake profile information (Mockaroo).

Automated Engagement:
TrollWall.ai – Automates content moderation and engagement (TrollWall.ai).
Thryv – Automates mass interactions (Thryv).

Deepfake Creation:
DeepSwap – AI-powered face-swapping technology (DeepSwap).
Synthesia IO – Converts text into AI-generated videos (Synthesia IO).
DeepFaceLab – Open-source tool for deepfake creation (DeepFaceLab).

These tools, originally for legitimate purposes, have been weaponized by bad actors for propaganda and political warfare.

Step-by-Step Example of a Troll Farm Operation:
1. Creating Fake Identities using Faker.js and Mockaroo.
2. Generating Fake Content using language models.
3. Automating Engagement using TrollWall.ai and Thryv.
4. Weaponizing Deepfakes using DeepSwap or Synthesia IO.
5. Suppressing Opponents via mass reporting.

This multi-layered approach allows troll farms to manipulate public discourse on an industrial scale.

The 2025 Elections: What’s at Stake?
2025 will be different. AI-powered troll farms will refine, personalize, and automate deception on a scale never seen before. For voters, distinguishing truth from lies will be harder than ever.

A Testing Ground for AI-Driven Manipulation:
Troll farms have perfected tactics to Manufacture Public Perception, Destroy Opponents, Hijack Trending Topics, and Suppress Dissenting Opinions. AI doesn’t need to convince everyone—it just needs to create enough confusion and division to make voters doubt what’s real.

The Cost of Letting AI-Generated Lies Take Over:
✔ A Leadership Built on Lies
✔ A More Divided Nation
✔ A Future Where Truth Doesn’t Matter

What Filipinos Must Do to Defend the Elections:
Voters must be skeptical, fact-checking must become a habit, candidates must call out disinformation, and Filipinos must refuse to let AI dictate their vote. This is the first major election where AI will play a decisive role. If Filipinos fail to recognize the threat, AI-driven disinformation will only get worse.
388:
You ever had that moment when you’re dead sure about something—something you saw, something you heard, something you lived through—only to be told, “Hindi naman ‘yan totoo.”

At first, you brush it off. Maybe they just remember things differently. Maybe you are being dramatic. Maybe you are overreacting.

But then it happens again. And again.

History books start changing. The crimes you grew up learning about suddenly didn’t happen. The suffering you saw with your own eyes becomes “fake news.” The leaders who once ruled with an iron fist? They were misunderstood. Biktima lang sila ng media.

You tell yourself, No, that’s not right. I remember. I know what happened.

But the louder voices drown you out. The TV, the radio, your own neighbors—everybody is telling you the same thing. And you start to wonder. “Baka nga mali ako.”

That’s gaslighting. Not in a toxic relationship. Not from a manipulative partner. But from the very people running our country.

Gaslighting thrives in the dark, and the only way to fight it is to drag it into the light. The more we expose it, the less power it has. The sad reality is that many Filipinos don’t even recognize political gaslighting for what it is. There’s a long academic name to explain it—but let’s be honest, how many will actually read it?

### Manipulation, Gaslighting, and Control: A Process of Political Power

I showed this draft to my 17-year-old, and she had one reaction: “You're using gaslighting, control, and manipulation interchangeably.” And she’s right. She’s smarter than me, and she caught something important—these aren’t the same thing, but they work together in a process.

Gaslighting is just one piece of a larger system. Politicians don’t just deceive—they make people question their own sanity until they give up resisting. It’s not just about lying; it’s about conditioning people to accept the lie as truth.

Manipulation is the broader strategy—the plan to distort public perception. Gaslighting is a specific psychological tool that makes people doubt what they see, hear, and remember. And when it works, the result is control—a population too uncertain or exhausted to push back.

### The Slow Unraveling: Seeing Gaslighting in Action

It starts small.

A Facebook post here, a casual comment there. Someone says Martial Law wasn’t that bad. That the country was more disciplined back then. That the streets were safe.

You scroll past it, shaking your head. People are really out here rewriting history.

But then, you hear it from your uncle. From your coworker. From the tricycle driver you casually chat with. The same narrative, word for word.

And then you notice something else—whenever someone speaks up, whenever someone questions it, they’re immediately shut down.

“Drama mo naman.”
“Puro ka reklamo.”
“Bayaran ka ba?”

The message is clear: Stop asking questions. Stop doubting the official story. Stop thinking for yourself.

It’s frustrating. And confusing. Because you know the facts are out there. The testimonies. The documents. The bodies.

But none of that seems to matter. Because those in power have mastered the art of making Filipinos doubt reality.

And it’s not just the politicians doing it. It’s the people around you—your friends, your family, even strangers on social media.

I have this friend on Facebook—a hardliner Duterte supporter. He will post anything he thinks supports his side. And he hates Marcos. One day, he shared a video of Prof. Cielo Magno questioning the national budget. I had to laugh—he genuinely thought Prof. Cielo Magno was pro-Duterte and anti-Marcos. He didn’t realize that the same professor had been critical of Duterte’s administration, too.

That’s how gaslighting spreads—not just from the top, but from ordinary people conditioned to see everything in black and white. If a politician they like is questioned, they dismiss it. If a politician they dislike is criticized, they cheer. It’s not just about control from above—it’s about the millions of voices that unknowingly keep the cycle going.

And before you know it, they’ve made Filipinos believe:

❌ “That never happened.” (Martial Law wasn’t that bad.)
❌ “You’re just overreacting.” (Extrajudicial killings? This was the time I felt safety and security for me and my family.)
❌ “It’s your fault.” (You’re poor because you’re lazy.)
❌ “Everyone else is lying.” (Journalists? Bayaran. Historians? Dilawan. Critics? Rebelde.)

And if they say it long enough and loud enough, people start to believe it. Even worse, they stop believing anything at all.

Gaslighting is just like a demon in an exorcism: it loses power the moment you name it. The second you recognize what’s happening, it stops working the way they want it to. That’s why exposing it is so important. The more we call it out, the weaker it becomes.

### The Election Cycle of Manipulation: Why We Keep Falling for It

Every election, we tell ourselves, “Hindi na tayo magpapaloko.” And yet, somehow, the same tactics work. Over and over again.

Why? Because political gaslighting doesn’t just happen during campaigns—it’s a slow, long-term conditioning.

### Step 5: Weaponizing Fear—Marcos Jr.'s Budget Shutdown Statement

Sometimes, gaslighting doesn’t stop once elections are won—it continues to justify decisions and suppress dissent.

Take, for example, President Marcos Jr.’s recent statement regarding the 2025 budget controversy. When the Supreme Court was asked to rule on the budget’s constitutionality, Marcos warned: “If the Supreme Court says, 'No, that budget is no longer in effect,' we shut down everything.”

This is a perfect example of political gaslighting—it forces the public into false urgency by making it seem like there are only two options:

✅ Support the budget → Everything runs smoothly
❌ Challenge it → The government collapses

It’s a classic manipulation tactic: make people believe that questioning the budget is dangerous, irresponsible, or even unpatriotic.

But here’s the truth—even if the Supreme Court ruled against the budget, the government wouldn’t actually shut down. It could still operate using a reenacted budget from the previous year.

So why frame it this way? To silence critics before they even speak.

Because if people are too afraid to challenge the government, those in power can do whatever they want—without accountability.

What makes gaslighting even more dangerous is that it doesn’t just come from politicians. It seeps into everyday conversations, passed around like secondhand smoke—barely noticed but just as toxic. Misinformation spreads not because it’s convincing, but because people repeat it without thinking. The real tragedy? Those who recognize the deception often stay quiet, watching as the cycle continues. And in the end, silence becomes complicity.

### Reclaiming Our Truth

Political gaslighting, manipulation, historical revisionism, conditioning—call it whatever you want, but recognize it for what it is. Whether it comes from leaders, the media, or even people around you, acknowledging it is the first step to breaking free from its grip. Because silence only feeds the cycle, and the only way to stop it is to expose it.

Gaslighting only works when people accept it. And we don’t have to.

We can question. We can fact-check. We can choose to see clearly, no matter how much they try to blind us.

Because the truth is more than just facts. It’s memory. It’s lived experience. It’s the stories passed down by those who refuse to forget.

And the moment we refuse to forget, they lose their power over us.

So let’s remember. Let’s keep talking. Let’s refuse to be silenced.

Because at the end of the day, truth isn’t a political stance—it’s our right.

And it’s time we fight for it.
389:
The Zero-Sum Illusion: Are Filipinos Wired to Lose?

I watched the steam rise from my coffee, curling and twisting like it was trying to escape something. It’s strange, isn’t it? The way something as simple as coffee knows how to let go, to share its warmth with the air until there’s nothing left but a cold cup.

Filipinos love to talk about *bayanihan*, about helping one another, about how we are one of the most hospitable people in the world. But if that’s true, why does it so often feel like we’re fighting each other just to survive? Why do we celebrate someone’s downfall instead of their success? Why does winning in this country often mean someone else has to lose?

### Where It All Started
Maybe it’s in our history. Before humans built civilizations, survival was a fight for limited resources—food, territory, mates. The world *was* zero-sum back then. But in today’s society, wealth and opportunities *can* grow. Yet, we still act like Neanderthals—competing for dominance, clinging to outdated instincts of survival, and tearing each other down instead of working together.

As a nation, we continue these same patterns—fighting over political power, treating economic progress as a competition rather than a shared goal, and seeing success as something to be hoarded rather than multiplied.

Colonization taught us that power was something to be taken, not shared. For centuries, a small elite held wealth and control while the majority struggled. —it was drilled into us, passed down through generations, reinforced by systems that made sure only a few could truly rise.

It’s the same mentality behind *crab mentality*, the habit of pulling others down when they try to climb up. It’s why, instead of asking how someone succeeded, many Filipinos whisper about how they must have cheated their way there. It’s why every election season feels less like a chance to choose good leaders and more like a brutal fight where the only goal is to defeat the other side.

This is what’s known as **zero-sum thinking**: the belief that one person’s gain must come at another’s expense, leading to a mindset of constant competition and distrust. But does this thinking really serve us? Or are we just playing a game where no one actually wins?

### Where It Shows Up in Everyday Life
*   **Politics** – Elections aren’t about governance, they’re about power struggles. Political opponents don’t just disagree—they destroy each other. And when someone wins? The other side spends years trying to tear them down instead of moving forward together.
*   **Electorate’s Political Immaturity** – Many Filipinos do not vote based on competence or leadership quality, but on personality, name recall, and popularity. Too often, elections become a spectacle rather than a rational process of electing the best leaders. Instead of assessing platforms and governance skills, voters are swayed by catchy slogans, celebrity endorsements, and emotional appeals, keeping unqualified politicians in power.
*   **Employment & Job Security** – Some bosses refuse to train employees too well, fearing they’ll take their skills elsewhere. Instead of developing people, companies hoard knowledge, creating an endless cycle of stagnation.
*   **Education & Career Paths** – Schools rank students against each other. Top graduates fight over the few high-paying jobs available. The job market isn’t about talent—it’s about connections, making the playing field anything but fair.
*   **OFW Culture** – We celebrate overseas Filipino workers as heroes, but what does that say about our system? Instead of creating opportunities here, we treat migration as the only way to survive. And when one family member succeeds abroad, the expectation is often that they must carry the entire household financially—sometimes at the cost of their own well-being.

### The Cost of This Thinking
What’s the result? Studies show that people with strong zero-sum beliefs tend to be more anxious and distrustful ([Blavatnik School of Government](https://www.bsg.ox.ac.uk/sites/default/files/2021-02/SM-WP-2021-001%20Zero-sum%20mindset%20and%20its%20discontents.pdf)). They see others’ success as a threat rather than an opportunity to learn or collaborate.

This explains why Filipinos often suspect successful individuals of cheating, rather than admiring their hard work. A country where progress is slow, where trust is low, where many of our best minds leave because they see no future here. A place where people believe that if they don’t take what they can now, they may never get another chance.

It tells us that for one person to win, another must lose. But that’s not how progress works. The world isn’t built on one person beating another—it thrives on cooperation, on people lifting each other up.

### What If We Stopped Playing This Game?
We’ve seen glimpses of what’s possible when we reject zero-sum thinking. Community efforts during typhoons, neighborhood *sari-sari* stores supporting each other instead of competing, local businesses thriving because they collaborate rather than undercutting each other.

Other countries have built their economies on this kind of cooperation. Japan, for example, didn’t become an economic powerhouse by tearing each other down—it was discipline, unity, and shared progress that got them there. Singapore, despite its size, became a global leader by investing in its people, not just a select few.

So why can’t we do the same?

Singapore wasn’t always rich—it focused on education and discipline, proving that success isn’t a limited pie. Lee Kuan Yew once said, "I do not believe that democracy necessarily leads to development. I believe that a country needs to develop is discipline more than democracy."

While his views may be controversial, Singapore’s transformation from a struggling nation to an economic powerhouse is proof of what focused governance and collective discipline can achieve.

South Korea invested in digital and economic cooperation, transforming itself from a war-torn nation into a global economic powerhouse. Their focus on innovation, education, and strategic partnerships allowed them to break free from poverty and create sustainable progress. Prioritizing collective growth over cutthroat competition, they proved that a country doesn't have to play a zero-sum game to succeed.

The Philippines is rich in talent and resources, but without a shift in mindset, those strengths will never translate into real progress. If we’re stuck fighting over scraps instead of building, we’ll never move forward.

### The Choice We Have to Make
It’s easy to say that Filipinos are wired to lose. After all, history has shaped our habits—centuries of colonization and survival-mode living don’t disappear overnight.

I am reminded of what my friend, a priest, once told me—'We are born to die, but what we do in between defines who we are and what we leave behind.'

Wiring isn’t destiny. We are not doomed to repeat the same mistakes forever. We see proof of this in Filipinos who thrive abroad, in communities that work together despite the odds, and in leaders—though rare—who prioritize nation-building over personal gain.

So the question isn’t whether we are wired to lose—it’s whether we’re willing to break free from the thinking that holds us back. The choice to rise—or to remain stuck—is ours.

We’ve spent centuries believing that for someone to succeed, another must suffer. But maybe we don’t have to keep playing by those rules. What if success wasn’t about taking but about creating? What if wealth wasn’t about hoarding but about expanding? What if power wasn’t about control but about collaboration?

History has already shown us what happens when we cling to the zero-sum mindset. Maybe it’s time to stop repeating the same mistakes.

I sip my coffee. It’s cooler now, the steam barely visible. It let go of its heat without a fight. And yet, it’s still coffee. Still good. Maybe we could learn something from that.

### A Call to Action: The Election is Upon Us
We have an opportunity to change things around. In a few weeks, we will once again choose our leaders. We have a choice: to pick the same people who keep us fighting for scraps, or to find those who believe in growing the pie for everyone.

Only we can save ourselves.
390:
Balota and the Philippine Curse of Repeating History

Balota isn’t just a film—it’s a reflection of the never-ending cycle of Philippine elections. Corruption, vote-buying, and political violence repeat like a bad script, and we, the audience, have seen it all before. In this personal reflective review, I dive into how the movie mirrors real-life struggles, how it reminds me of my adoptive mother—a teacher once caught in the web of election duty—and why that final Putang ina isn’t just a line, but the collective frustration of an entire nation. Will this film wake us up, or will history repeat itself once again?

I’ve seen this before.

Not on the big screen. Not in some award-winning political thriller.

I’ve seen this in real life.

Every election season, the same script plays out. Violence erupts. Vote-buying runs rampant. Candidates make empty promises while the powerless watch, hoping—maybe this time, it will be different.

It never is.

Balota

 hits too close to home. It’s a film, but it may as well be a documentary. The chaos, the corruption, the helplessness—it’s all too familiar. And for me, it’s personal.

My adoptive mother was a teacher. Though she never had to flee into the forest with a ballot box like Emmy did, she carried the weight of election duty on her shoulders for many years. Teachers don’t sign up for this fight, yet every election, they are forced into the crossfire. They become reluctant guardians of democracy, trying to protect a system that never protects them back.

That’s what 

Balota

 gets right. The suffocating fear. The desperation. The bitter truth that no matter how hard you fight, history repeats itself.

And when Emmy screams that final, searing word—

Putang ina!

—it doesn’t feel like fiction.

It feels like a nation screaming with her.

The Reality Behind the Film

This isn’t just a movie.

This is the Philippines, every election year.

We’ve heard the stories. Teachers dragged into election chaos, risking their lives for a system that treats them as collateral. Some threatened. Some bribed. Some never made it home.

My mother was never caught in the middle of a gunfight, but I remember the tension. The unspoken fear. The weight she carried every time she was assigned to election duty.

She wasn’t just a teacher on those days. She was a witness. A reluctant participant in a game where the rules were written by those in power.

In 
Balota
, Emmy is that witness. She runs through the forest, ballot box in hand, chased by men who don’t care about democracy—only victory. It’s absurd. It’s terrifying. And it’s real.

One scene refuses to leave my mind.

A group of goons, their hands clasped together, heads bowed in prayer. They make the sign of the cross, murmuring blessings before stepping out to spill blood in the name of their candidate.

This is who we’ve become. A nation that prays before it kills. A people who ask for divine protection before committing the very sins we condemn on Sundays.

Election violence isn’t an anomaly. It’s tradition.

The cycle continues because people let it. Vote-buying isn’t just done in the shadows anymore. It happens in broad daylight, wrapped in cheap promises and crisp bills slipped into eager hands.

Everyone knows. No one stops it.

And every time, we convince ourselves that maybe, just maybe, things will change.

They don’t.

The Ending: A Punch in the Gut

It all comes back to that final scene.

Emmy, exhausted. Bloodied. Eyes hollow from everything she has endured. The ballot box—the symbol of democracy, of hope, of everything she fought for—now just a battered, meaningless object.

The news plays in the background.

The same headlines. The same faces. The same empty outrage.

Nothing has changed.

And then, she says it.

Putang ina.

Sharp. Raw. A curse, not just for herself, but for all of us. For this country that never learns. For the endless cycle of corruption, of stolen elections, of people who cry foul one day and forget the next.

I felt that. Deep in my bones.

Because that’s the thing about 
Balota
. It doesn’t end with victory. There are no triumphant speeches, no moment of redemption. Just that one bitter, searing word.

A scream of frustration that echoes beyond the screen.

Because we all know what happens next.

The cycle begins again.

Will This Film Wake People Up?

That’s the real question, isn’t it?

Will 
Balota
 shake people enough to finally say, 
Tama na
? Or will it become just another film—watched, praised, then forgotten when the next election circus comes around?

Filipinos have seen it all. The ballot-snatching. The vote-buying. The killings. The parades of plastic smiles, campaign jingles, and promises that mean nothing. We rage about it online. We joke about it. We call out the corruption while laughing at the very people who enable it.

And then, we move on.

That’s why the film hurts. Because it holds up a mirror, and what we see isn’t just broken politics—it’s us.

We let this happen. Again and again.

But maybe, just maybe, 
Balota
 will plant a seed. Maybe someone will watch it and feel that same boiling frustration. Maybe, for once, we won’t just laugh off the insanity of our elections.

Maybe we’ll finally stop waiting for things to change on their own.

Or maybe, when the next election comes, we’ll hear the same news. See the same violence. Watch the same cycle play out.

And all we’ll have left is another 
Putang ina.

Conclusion: Breaking the Cycle

We say we want change.

But do we?

Every election, we cry out against corruption, against vote-buying, against the same recycled names running the show. Yet when the ballots are cast, the same faces win. The same families tighten their grip. The same headlines flash across our screens.

Nothing changes.

That’s why 
Balota
 isn’t just a film—it’s a warning. It forces us to ask the hard questions: 
When will we stop accepting this as normal? When will we stop laughing at our own suffering? When will we finally decide that enough is enough?

I think about my mother. A teacher who, like many others, was thrown into election duty without a choice. I wonder if she ever felt the same frustration Emmy did. If she ever questioned whether what she was doing mattered, if she ever saw the cycle repeating even then.

I don’t want to keep asking these same questions decades from now.

The film ends with a scream of anger, a word that sums up years of exhaustion, betrayal, and hopelessness. But maybe, just maybe, it doesn’t have to be the final word.

Maybe the next election, we won’t just watch. We won’t just rage online.

Maybe this time, we’ll fight.

Maybe this time, we’ll break the cycle.
391:
Loving a country isn’t blind loyalty—it’s demanding better. But how long can hope survive when the Philippines settles for less and refuses to change?

They tell me my words are too harsh. Too negative. That I should write about hope instead. That if I have so many complaints about the Philippines, I should just leave. As if loving your country means pretending it isn’t falling apart. As if the only way to show patriotism is through blind loyalty, not righteous anger. As if pointing out what is broken makes you less of a Filipino, when in truth, it’s the silence that hurts most.

I do not hate the Philippines. I hate how Filipinos have been conditioned to accept so little. To celebrate what should be expected. To lower their standards so much that even a drop of decency from a politician feels like an act of grace.

A road gets paved? "Wow, ang galing niya." Never mind that the funding for that project was taken from taxpayers. A new law is passed? "Salamat sa pagmamalasakit." But wasn't that their job to begin with? Filipinos clap for leaders who simply do what they were elected to do. A mayor cleans up a city, and suddenly he is hailed as a savior. A senator passes one good law, and people act as if they owe him their loyalty for life. A president fulfills a campaign promise, and his supporters demand unwavering gratitude—never mind the thousand others left broken.

They defend corruption with passion. They see criticism as an attack, rather than an opportunity to demand better. The bar is so low that even mediocrity is applauded. And when you ask for more? When you say, "We deserve better"? You are met with hostility. "Eh di lumayas ka kung ayaw mo dito." As if expecting competence is too much. As if wanting a country that protects its people, that values their dignity, that does not exploit their resilience, is an act of treason.

We laugh at our problems. Because what else is there to do? Gas prices soar, and we joke about needing bicycles. Traffic gets worse, and we turn it into a running gag. Government officials steal from public funds, and we turn them into punchlines rather than demanding accountability. We turn our tragedies into memes, our hardships into running jokes. We make light of everything until the weight of it no longer registers. "Wala na tayong magagawa."

But there is always something that can be done. The problem is, too many are unwilling. Too many are comfortable in their discomfort, unwilling to shake the foundations of a system that thrives on their submissiveness. And those who do speak up? They are ridiculed and dismissed, their voices drowned out by those who refuse to face the truth.

Loving a country is not just about singing the anthem with pride. In other nations, leaders are scrutinized, held accountable, and even forced to step down when they fail. Citizens demand transparency, question policies, and push for systemic reforms—understanding that critique is an essential part of progress, not a reason to be silenced. Here, questioning authority is seen as betrayal, when in reality, it is the highest form of patriotism. It is not about waving the flag or waxing poetic about resilience. Love should not be blind. If you truly love something, you do not let it rot. You do not allow it to keep making the same mistakes. You do not turn your back when it chooses the wrong path.

So yes, I will continue to write about what is wrong. Not because I have given up, but because I still have hope—no matter how tired I am. Because love, real love, does not mean silence. It means fighting for change, even when the fight feels impossible.

But how long can hope survive in a country that refuses to change? The answer is up to us. Hope fades when we stay silent, when we accept scraps instead of justice, when we let them convince us that things will never change. But hope endures when we refuse to be complacent. When we demand more. When we remind those in power that they serve us—not the other way around.
392:
DeepSeek AI, a rapidly growing Chinese startup, has made waves in the tech world with its cost-efficient artificial intelligence models. However, the company’s swift rise has sparked serious concerns among U.S. cybersecurity experts and policymakers, who view DeepSeek as a potential national security risk. These fears echo the debates surrounding the social media platform TikTok, drawing parallels in how both companies handle data security and governmental influence.

## What is DeepSeek AI and How Does It Work?

Founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Zhejiang University graduate and former hedge fund manager, DeepSeek AI aimed to revolutionize artificial intelligence. Unlike major Western tech companies like OpenAI and Google, which rely on massive datasets and costly hardware, DeepSeek takes a different approach. It employs a “Mixture of Experts” (MoE) architecture, which delivers high performance with far less computational power and expense.

This unique methodology has drawn attention across the AI industry, with DeepSeek’s models showing performance comparable to top-tier competitors but at a fraction of the cost. The company’s open-source framework allows startups and individual developers to access advanced AI models, enabling a wider array of innovation and development. For organizations with limited resources, this affordability opens the door to breakthroughs in critical areas such as healthcare, education, and research.

## National Security Concerns and DeepSeek AI

Despite its impressive technological advances, DeepSeek AI’s origins and practices have prompted scrutiny from U.S. officials. Concerns revolve around data storage on servers in China and the country’s cybersecurity laws, which require companies to share data with government authorities. Critics warn that sensitive user information—such as personal communications and chat histories—could potentially be accessed and exploited for espionage or influence campaigns.

Additionally, DeepSeek’s AI models have proven vulnerable to “jailbreak” techniques that allow users to bypass safety filters and generate harmful content. This has raised fears that malicious actors could misuse the technology to spread disinformation or create harmful materials. Together, these vulnerabilities and the potential for government access have fueled calls to assess the risks of adopting DeepSeek’s technology in the U.S. and other Western countries.

## Comparing DeepSeek AI and TikTok: What’s the Difference?

The concerns surrounding DeepSeek AI have drawn comparisons to TikTok, the Chinese-owned social media platform that has faced scrutiny over data security and government influence. Like TikTok, DeepSeek is suspected of exposing American data to the Chinese government, raising alarms over national security. However, the two differ in their scope and potential impact.

While TikTok’s primary concern lies in the collection of user preferences and behavioral data, DeepSeek AI’s capabilities are far more vast. Its advanced AI models mean it can access a much wider range of information, from personal details to sensitive corporate data. This more extensive data collection amplifies fears that DeepSeek could be used for competitive intelligence or to target critical infrastructure. Moreover, its open-source approach makes it harder to regulate than TikTok, complicating efforts to manage the risks associated with its adoption.

## Expert Opinions on DeepSeek AI’s Risks

Opinions among experts vary regarding the potential dangers posed by DeepSeek AI. Some cybersecurity professionals argue that its vulnerabilities to malicious manipulations, such as jailbreak techniques, represent a significant threat. They fear that this could allow bad actors to repurpose the technology for harmful purposes, including financial fraud or cyberattacks. There’s also concern that sensitive corporate or governmental data entered into DeepSeek’s models could be harvested and exploited by Chinese authorities.

On the other hand, some analysts argue that these risks are overblown, noting that American AI companies also face scrutiny over user data. They suggest that instead of focusing solely on the risks, the U.S. should use the rise of DeepSeek as a catalyst to invest in domestic AI innovation and improve regulatory frameworks. In this view, fostering competition and establishing clear guidelines for responsible AI use could help address security concerns without stifling progress.

## How the U.S. is Responding

In light of the security concerns surrounding DeepSeek AI, the U.S. government is taking steps to address the issue. The National Security Council (NSC) has launched an investigation into the technology’s implications. Major agencies have already banned the use of DeepSeek’s tools, citing security and ethical concerns. Lawmakers have also proposed tighter export controls on key components, such as the Nvidia H800 chip, to limit DeepSeek’s access to critical resources.

Some officials are pushing to update Federal Acquisition Regulations to prevent federal agencies from using data processed on Chinese platforms like DeepSeek. Additionally, a growing number of companies and government agencies are implementing bans on employee access to DeepSeek’s models. These measures reflect a broader strategy to minimize exposure to potential data breaches and mitigate security risks associated with foreign AI technologies.

## DeepSeek’s Impact on Global AI Competition

The rapid rise of DeepSeek AI marks a turning point in the global AI landscape. By delivering cutting-edge performance at lower costs, DeepSeek challenges the long-standing dominance of Western tech giants. This shift could lead to a more fragmented AI ecosystem, where adoption depends as much on international alliances as on technological superiority.

DeepSeek’s cost-effective approach also forces other companies to reconsider their strategies. Western firms may need to innovate more aggressively or lower costs to stay competitive. At the same time, governments may need to strike a balance between balancing the benefits of international collaboration with the need to protect national security interests. As DeepSeek continues to grow, its presence will likely reshape the global AI industry, compelling stakeholders around the world to adapt to this new reality.

## Economic and Infrastructure Implications

DeepSeek AI’s cost-efficient models could have far-reaching effects on both energy consumption and infrastructure needs. While the company’s approach significantly lowers the computing resources needed for individual tasks, the sheer volume of AI use is expected to drive overall energy usage upward. The growing popularity of AI applications, from healthcare to finance, will likely lead to increased demands for infrastructure to support this expansion.

This shift underscores the importance of continued investment in energy resources, data centers, and advanced computing infrastructure. Without these upgrades, the AI industry could face bottlenecks that stifle innovation and growth. DeepSeek’s emergence goes beyond technical performance—it’s a call to adapt our infrastructure and energy policies to meet the needs of an increasingly AI-driven future.

## How DeepSeek AI Responds to Security Concerns

DeepSeek AI has publicly acknowledged the concerns raised by U.S. officials and has reiterated its commitment to protecting user data. The company asserts that its AI models are developed and deployed in compliance with international security standards and that it has implemented robust safeguards to protect against misuse. Additionally, DeepSeek highlights its open-source philosophy as a way to promote transparency and foster innovation across the AI community.

Despite these assurances, critics remain skeptical. They argue that as long as DeepSeek’s data storage and operations are governed by Chinese cybersecurity laws, there will always be questions about how secure the data truly is. While DeepSeek’s transparency efforts may offer some reassurance, they have not fully alleviated fears about potential data exposure or governmental influence. As the conversation around DeepSeek continues, the company’s ability to address these issues in a meaningful way will play a critical role in determining its long-term acceptance and success on the global stage.

## Conclusion: Striking a Balance Between Innovation and Security

The emergence of DeepSeek AI highlights a crucial challenge: how to embrace groundbreaking technology while safeguarding national security. On one hand, DeepSeek’s cost-effective, open-source models offer immense potential for innovation across various regions and industries. On the other, its Chinese roots and data storage practices have understandably raised alarms among U.S. officials and cybersecurity experts.

Addressing these concerns requires a balanced approach. Excessive restrictions might stifle innovation and prevent valuable technologies from flourishing, while a lack of oversight could leave critical infrastructure and sensitive data vulnerable. A collaborative effort among governments, industry leaders, and researchers is essential to establish clear safety standards, promote responsible AI development, and mitigate potential risks.

DeepSeek’s rise is a reminder that the AI landscape is constantly evolving. As new technologies emerge, we must remain vigilant, adaptive, and open to dialogue—ensuring that innovation can thrive without compromising security.
393:
DeepSeek, a relatively unknown Chinese AI startup, has suddenly become a major player in the AI industry, sending shockwaves through Silicon Valley. Its latest open-source model, DeepSeek-R1, has sparked concerns due to its capabilities that rival those of established giants like OpenAI and Google. But how did DeepSeek rise to prominence so quickly?

Founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, DeepSeek emerged from the research division of High-Flyer, a Chinese quantitative hedge fund. With a strong focus on artificial general intelligence (AGI), DeepSeek quickly gained traction as a tech firm with ambitious goals.

Its AI models have already made an impact, with DeepSeek-Coder, launched in November 2023, providing advanced support for coding tasks. However, it was the release of DeepSeek-R1 in January 2025 that brought the startup into the spotlight. This reasoning-based model, developed using Nvidia H800 chips at a fraction of the cost of OpenAI’s O1, delivered performance comparable to its more expensive counterpart.

DeepSeek’s approach emphasizes efficiency and open-source accessibility, challenging the belief that developing advanced AI requires vast computational resources. Techniques such as inference-time computing and optimized communication bandwidth enable DeepSeek models to perform effectively with limited hardware requirements. This has prompted a reevaluation of AI development strategies worldwide. Despite these advancements, further technical details on the specific architectural improvements of DeepSeek-R1 remain scarce, leaving many in the AI community curious about the depth of its innovations.

Operating within China’s regulatory framework, DeepSeek avoids discussing politically sensitive topics. Queries about the Tiananmen Square massacre or Taiwan’s political status are either sidestepped or answered in alignment with Chinese government narratives. While this approach ensures compliance within China, it raises ethical questions about AI and information control. This also opens discussions about AI regulation globally—whether governments should implement similar oversight or allow unfiltered access to AI-generated responses.

On January 27, 2025, Nvidia’s stock dropped by 17%, erasing nearly $600 billion in market value. Investors expressed concerns over how DeepSeek’s cost-effective models could disrupt the demand for high-end AI chips, which are central to Nvidia’s business. However, Nvidia was not the only company affected. Semiconductor manufacturers such as AMD and Intel also saw minor dips in stock prices, signaling a potential shift in how investors view the AI industry's reliance on cutting-edge hardware.

DeepSeek’s emergence has also forced established tech firms to reconsider their AI development approaches. The model of massive investments in proprietary models may shift towards more sustainable and open-source alternatives. Apple, often criticized for lagging in AI development, could leverage DeepSeek’s methodologies to develop advanced AI without heavy investments in infrastructure. While Apple has been exploring on-device AI applications, DeepSeek’s breakthroughs may push the company to adopt similar lightweight, hardware-efficient techniques, potentially allowing Apple to integrate high-performing AI models directly into its devices without cloud dependency.

DeepSeek’s rise despite U.S. export restrictions highlights the resilience of Chinese AI innovation. This development raises questions about the effectiveness of trade controls and underscores the shifting balance of power in global AI leadership. In response, the U.S. and the European Union may reassess their AI regulatory strategies, potentially leading to new policies around data sovereignty and AI development transparency. Discussions about AI national security are already taking place, with some policymakers proposing greater scrutiny of Chinese AI models operating in Western markets.

Similar to controversies surrounding TikTok, DeepSeek’s origins have led to national security concerns. The platform reportedly blocks criticism of the Chinese Communist Party, further fueling debates about censorship and potential government influence. Moreover, cyberattacks targeting DeepSeek have surfaced, reflecting the competitive and politically charged nature of the AI race. It remains unclear whether these cyberattacks originated from state-sponsored actors, or independent hacking groups, but they highlight the vulnerabilities even the most advanced firms face.

With its democratization of AI through open-source models, efficiency-driven approach, and the potential fragmentation of AI development into different specialized areas, DeepSeek is reshaping the industry. Lower costs could encourage competition and innovation among smaller AI players. However, this fragmentation raises new concerns: will tech companies focus on developing general-purpose AI while smaller firms build highly specialized, industry-specific models? If so, the AI landscape may shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach toward an ecosystem of diverse, optimized solutions.

DeepSeek also aims to develop AGI, pushing AI capabilities beyond specific tasks to broader cognitive functions. However, many experts remain skeptical about whether DeepSeek has the resources to achieve such a goal. Compared to OpenAI, DeepMind, or Anthropic, DeepSeek has a smaller research footprint. However, its efficiency-driven design philosophy could enable it to leapfrog traditional AGI development challenges by focusing on optimizing cognitive workflows rather than increasing model size.

One of the biggest unanswered questions surrounding DeepSeek is its long-term business model. While its open-source framework has garnered praise, sustaining a company without clear revenue streams could be difficult. Will DeepSeek pivot towards enterprise AI services? Will it seek cloud-based monetization strategies, similar to OpenAI’s GPT API? Or will it depend on governmental funding to stay afloat? The long-term sustainability of its operations is a subject of debate.

DeepSeek’s emergence challenges conventional AI development, shifting the focus towards efficiency and accessibility. While its long-term impact remains uncertain, its innovations are already prompting a reassessment of AI strategies worldwide. The implications extend beyond technology, influencing global AI governance, market dynamics, and ethical discussions. Whether DeepSeek will continue to disrupt the industry or fade into obscurity remains to be seen, but for now, the AI world is watching closely.

For the longest time, I believed my hearing was invincible. Years of work with headsets strapped on during my early days in a call center career never made me think twice about my ears. Add to that my habit of wearing earphones while traveling or doing laundry—it was second nature. What could possibly go wrong?

But hearing loss doesn’t happen all at once. It sneaks up on you, like an uninvited guest who overstays their welcome. At first, it was just a struggle to understand conversations. I could hear people talking, but the words were muffled, as if they were fading into nothing. It was frustrating, but I brushed it off. I could see the frustration in my wife’s and daughter’s eyes whenever I asked them to repeat themselves. My father-in-law, who often speaks in a hushed voice, was almost impossible for me to understand, even if we were in the car and I was sitting right next to him.

I remember my adoptive mother reminding me to stop or limit the use of headsets because I was going to lose my hearing. I wasn’t listening, I never did. Now I know what she means.

## The Warning Signs

It wasn’t until the frustration became unbearable that I decided to get my hearing tested. Recently, I’ve noticed that even when my wife is standing right in front of me, I’m unable to understand most of what she is saying. I can hear the voices of others, but I keep asking them to repeat themselves. Strangers aren’t spared either; if I don’t understand them, I just nod—typical of a Filipino. The results hit me harder than expected. My right ear, thankfully, was still within normal range, but my left ear had mild sensorineural hearing loss—a progressive condition caused by years of exposure to loud sounds and noise. The audiologist explained that because my left ear has hearing loss, my right ear is overcompensating, which could lead to further damage if left unaddressed. The audiologist explained it, but what stuck with me was how easily this could have been avoided.

I remember sitting there as the audiologist walked me through the chart, pointing out dips in the frequencies that my left ear could no longer detect. It was sobering. I wasn’t just losing my hearing; I was losing pieces of the world—the muffled laughter of my daughter, the soft rustling of leaves, even the comforting hum of the world at night. I hadn’t even noticed how quiet things had become until the quiet was deafening.

The audiologist told me that there are typically two options—get a surgery or use a hearing aid. I asked the audiologist how much a hearing aid normally costs, and he casually mentioned between 25,000 to 160,000 pesos. I was in for a surprise. Surgery was out of the question for me. My experience with three surgeries related to sleep apnea left me with a terrible fear of going under the knife again. I had to find other options, and the best way I know how is through research.

They say Facebook doesn’t spy on you, but let’s be honest, it does. It’s always listening, because as soon as I talked about hearing aids, ads populated my news feed. It’s both good and bad—bad because Mark Zuckerberg and his team are clearly snooping on my conversations, and good because I needed an affordable hearing aid.

I sat there, staring at the results, feeling a mix of regret and resignation. But regret alone wouldn’t help. It was a turning point. I knew I needed to take action to regain the life I’d been slowly losing to silence and muffled sounds. I decided to start with a very cheap kind of hearing aid. The truth is, I was hesitant to invest in a premium model when I wasn’t sure if it really would be beneficial. I’ve read about how effective hearing aids can be, with plenty of research backing them up. Still, I wasn’t entirely convinced. I needed to see for myself if it would fit into my lifestyle and make a difference before committing to a more expensive and advanced model.

Expensive hearing aids often come with high-tech features like Bluetooth connectivity for seamless streaming from devices, noise-cancellation systems that adapt to different environments, and rechargeable batteries that eliminate the need for constant replacements. They can even be customized to match specific hearing needs, using AI to automatically adjust settings based on the sounds around you. These features sounded impressive, but I had to face the reality that there was no way around this—hearing aids and I are now inseparable partners. I needed to see if a hearing aid could truly make a difference in my daily life before making such a significant investment.

## A First-Time Hearing Aid User

Then came the hearing aid. For the first time in years, I could hear everything. And I mean everything. As I joked recently, “The good news is, I can hear everything. The bad news is, I can hear everything.”

The first day wearing it felt like stepping into a world that was too loud to be real. The clacking of keys on my keyboard sounded like tiny explosions. Tricycles from streets away sounded as if they were barreling down my hallway.

Driving was a surreal experience—I could hear the car engine humming, the tires rolling on asphalt, and even the jingle of loose change in the cup holder.

A construction site nearby made its presence known in the loudest way possible—the generator, the jackhammer, and even every metallic clang seemed to reverberate in my skull. The odd thing? I was also gaining access to conversations from people nearby. I could hear them even when I didn’t want to. These were sounds I hadn’t realized I’d been missing, but they were all rushing back in at once, overwhelmingly.

The change wasn’t just physical; it was emotional too. There were moments of joy—hearing my daughter’s laughter with crystal clarity, catching the chirp of a bird I hadn’t noticed before, or realizing I could now hear my wife when she was speaking from the kitchen. My daughter joked I could now hear my wife when she’s mad at me. And if she gets too loud or is nagging anymore, I could simply turn off the hearing aid. But there were also moments of frustration. The world is a noisy place, and the sounds were almost too sharp. Right now, on my second day, I’m still struggling with the adjustment because even at the lowest volume, everything is loud. I’m starting to think it now has something to do with the quality of the hearing aid. It’s a learning process, learning to live with the noise again, but it’s a process I’m grateful for.

## Listening to What Matters

If there’s one thing I’ve learned, it’s this: when someone warns you, it’s out of love. My mother’s voice echoes in my head now, louder than ever. I didn’t listen then, but I’m sharing this story to remind others to listen while they still can.

There’s a difference between hearing and listening. Hearing is simply perceiving sound, but listening involves attention, understanding, and care. You only realize the value of one when you are slowly losing the other. For years, I could hear everything, but I wasn’t really listening—to warnings, to advice, or even to the world around me. It’s ironic that I had to lose some of my hearing to finally appreciate the importance of truly listening.

As I reflect on this, I am reminded of a song by Freddie Aguilar that speaks volumes:

Ano sa 'yo ang musika, sa 'yo ba'y mahalaga
Matahimik mong paligid, awitan ay 'di madinig
Mapalad ka, o kaibigan, napakaingay ng mundo
Sa isang binging katulad mo, walang daing, walang gulo

Hearing loss might feel like a losing battle, but it’s one worth fighting. It’s not just about the sounds; it’s about the connections, the moments, the life you might miss out on. Take care of your ears. If you notice the signs, don’t ignore them. If your loved ones remind you to take a break, pay attention. They might just be saving you from regrets down the road.

For anyone who might be in the same position I was, take that first step. Get your hearing checked, use the aids, and listen to the world again. Don’t wait until it’s too late. Life is full of sounds waiting to be rediscovered. Sometimes, the noise is a blessing.

AI Content Writing & Skilled Writers Unite

We live in a time where AI can draft essays, write articles, and suggest ways to optimize content for search engines. It’s impressive, sure—but there’s something AI can’t replace: the human connection. That’s where content writers come in, bringing creativity and insight to create stories that readers truly care about.

AI can analyze numbers, spot trends, and even create a rough draft. But when it comes to understanding the heart of a story or making a message feel relatable, only a writer can do the job. Writers turn AI’s data into content that people remember. It’s not just about informing readers—it’s about leaving an impact.

For example, AI might propose writing about trending social media topics, but it takes a writer to add the emotional depth and nuance that makes the piece stand out. By working together, AI and content writers create something better than either could on their own.

How AI Fits into Content Creation

AI tools can simplify many parts of the writing process. Tasks like checking grammar, analyzing keywords, and brainstorming content ideas can now be done in minutes. This saves time and allows writers to focus on what matters most—crafting engaging and authentic content.

Data analysis is another area where AI excels. It can highlight what audiences are searching for, how they engage with certain topics, and what trends are worth exploring. Writers can use this information to create targeted content that answers real questions and provides value.

AI can also support writers with first drafts. For example, tools that generate rough outlines or initial ideas can help writers overcome blank-page syndrome. These drafts aren’t perfect but serve as a starting point that a human writer can refine into something meaningful.

Why Writers Are Still Essential

No matter how advanced AI becomes, it can’t replace the creativity and emotion of human writers. A great article isn’t just about presenting facts; it’s about making readers feel understood. Writers know how to connect with people by using language that feels personal and real.

Context is another thing AI struggles with. While it might understand data, it often misses cultural nuances, humor, and subtleties of tone. Writers can adapt their style to suit different audiences and situations, ensuring the message lands in the right way.

Originality is another reason writers remain vital. AI relies on patterns and existing data, which can lead to repetitive or generic ideas. Writers, on the other hand, can create fresh content by drawing from their experiences, observations, and imagination.

How AI and Writers Work Together

The best results come when AI and writers collaborate. AI takes care of repetitive or technical tasks, giving writers more time to focus on creative work. This partnership allows businesses to produce content faster without sacrificing quality.

For example, AI can suggest which keywords to use for better visibility or analyze how well previous content has performed. Writers can use this data to refine their work, making it more impactful. Together, they achieve a balance of efficiency and creativity that benefits both businesses and readers.

Personalization Through AI

AI’s ability to analyze user behavior makes personalization easier. It can help segment audiences based on their interests, habits, or demographics. With this information, writers can tailor their content to specific groups, creating a stronger connection that keeps readers engaged.

Tailored content isn’t just about the message but also the tone, style, and timing. AI tools can suggest the best time to share content for the best results. Writers use these insights to deliver material that feels relevant and timely, improving the overall experience for readers.

Overcoming Challenges with AI in Content Creation

While AI is a valuable tool, it comes with its challenges. AI-generated content can sometimes feel cold or robotic. This is why the writer’s role in adding personality and depth is so important. A well-told story or a unique perspective can turn ordinary content into something unforgettable.

There’s also the risk of relying too much on AI. While data is helpful, overanalyzing trends can make content feel forced or formulaic. Writers must strike a balance between following data and trusting their instincts to create content that truly stands out.

Finally, technology changes quickly, and staying updated can be overwhelming. Writers who embrace learning and adaptability will continue to thrive, using AI as a tool to enhance their skills rather than replace them.

Looking Ahead: AI and the Future of Writing

As AI continues to improve, it’s likely to play an even bigger role in content creation. Tools may become smarter, offering deeper insights into audience preferences or providing more creative suggestions. This will allow writers to push boundaries, creating meaningful stories while using AI to handle the technical side of things.

Emerging technologies like augmented reality and virtual reality will also create new opportunities for content creators. Imagine creating interactive experiences that allow readers to engage with stories in entirely new ways. While these technological advancements are exciting, one thing remains certain: the human touch will always be essential in creating content that matters.

AI and content writers aren’t competitors—they’re partners. Together, they bring the best of both worlds: efficiency and creativity, data and emotion. Businesses that embrace this partnership will find themselves better equipped to connect with their audiences in ways that feel authentic and impactful.

Ready to take your content strategy to the next level? Let’s explore how we can work together. Email me at inquiry@ginoborlado.org, and let’s start creating content that truly connects.
